summary: Audit Log — capture all PII override decisions automatically
description_md:
As a compliance-officer, I want the system to automatically capture all PII override decisions in the audit log so that I can maintain a complete, tamper-evident record of every manual classification change for regulatory compliance and forensic analysis.

## Background

The PII Auto-Tagging feature (F-B1) allows workspace-admins to override automatic classification decisions when the machine learning model misidentifies sensitive data. These override actions represent manual interventions that must be tracked to satisfy audit requirements under data protection regulations like GDPR and CCPA.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. When a workspace-admin overrides a PII classification via the F-B1 interface, an audit log entry is created within 500ms containing the user ID, timestamp, original classification, new classification, and data record identifier.

2. Each audit log entry for a PII override includes an immutable hash linking to the previous entry, preventing retroactive tampering.

3. The compliance-officer can filter the Audit Log Export (F-B2) by "override" action type and date range, returning all matching PII override entries.

4. Audit log entries for PII overrides include the workspace-admin's full name, email, and the specific data source (connector) where the override occurred.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Implementation complete. Hash chaining uses SHA-256. Verified with test data from Salesforce connector.
